customer_anxiety_profile: Quanisha is cautiously cooperative but carrying unresolved anxiety from a prior solar disqualification she can't explain. She's not the title-holder — her fiance is — and she's managing a busy household schedule. Her engagement is real (unprompted payment question at 04:54) but fragile: no locked appointment, no confirmed fiance availability, and the prior disqualification concern was never addressed.
unresolved_concerns: Customer does not know why she previously didn't qualify for solar — this was never resolved on the call and will surface again at the closer appointment if not addressed proactively
Fiance is the title-holder and was never confirmed as available for or aware of the appointment
Bill not yet received — soft commitment to send via text/email, no locked follow-up mechanism
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior solar disqualification (unknown company, unknown reason — customer said 'I'm not quite sure')
Fiance is title-holder, not Quanisha — appointment authority is with someone who was not on this call
Active duty or retired military status (fiance) — flagged by rep as relevant to program eligibility but not explored
exact_promises_rep_made: Rep told customer to 'make sure to bring up' military status at the appointment for potential program utilization
Rep said Ion would 'show a roof estimate' given the 20-year-old roof ('Won't add anything, really')

Rep implied the tax credit is still available ('there is a tax credit in place right now that's gonna be leaving at the end of the year') — note: codex flags that the tax credit can no longer be guaranteed on purchased systems
closer_should_know: The fiance is the title-holder and was not on this call — the appointment is structurally vulnerable until he is confirmed as a participant. Closer should open by confirming the fiance is present before proceeding. Additionally, the customer's prior disqualification (unknown company, unknown reason) is unresolved anxiety that will surface early; closer should address it proactively rather than waiting for it to become an objection.
